highlights: Built a canvas-style interface for chaining AI tasks across chat contexts and workflow nodes.
Structured the system to support multimodal generation paths instead of isolated one-off prompts.
Focused on operationalizing agent workflows so the UX feels like a command surface, not a demo.
impact: Established a strong foundation for multi-agent workflow composition with a distinct technical brand and interface language.
one_liner: Agentic engineering front-end that frames multi-agent workflows as a production command surface.
problem: Single-prompt tooling made it hard to compose repeatable AI workflows with shared context and reusable execution paths.
solution: Created a frontend system for node-based and chat-driven orchestration so users can structure, connect, and reuse AI execution flows.
title: genr8ive
visibility_note: Core repository is private. This entry summarizes architecture and working approach without exposing proprietary source.
